title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description:
You are given an array `prices` where `prices[i]` is the price of a given stock on the ith day.

You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy and a single day to sell. You must buy before you sell.

Return the maximum profit. If no profit is possible, return 0.

parts.prompt: Find the maximum profit from buying and selling a stock once. You must buy before you sell.
parts.internalNotes: Brute force is O(n²) checking all pairs. Optimal: track min price seen so far, compute profit at each step. Common mistakes: selling before buying, not handling decreasing prices.

hints.content: You only need to track one piece of information as you scan left to right.
What's the best price you could have bought at, for each sell day?
Track the minimum price seen so far. At each day, profit = price - minSoFar.

testCases.explanation: Prices only decrease, no profit possible

followUps: What's the time and space complexity?
parts.expectedApproach: Single pass tracking min price, compute max profit at each step.

transitionCriteria.transitionPrompt: Nice. Now what if you could make unlimited transactions? You can buy and sell multiple times, but must sell before buying again.

parts.prompt: Now you can make unlimited buy/sell transactions (but must sell before buying again). Maximize total profit.
parts.internalNotes: Greedy: add up all positive differences between consecutive days. Equivalent to buying every valley and selling every peak. Common mistake: trying to find peaks/valleys explicitly instead of using the simpler greedy.

hints.content: Think about what happens between any two consecutive days.
If tomorrow's price is higher than today's, what should you do?
Sum all positive differences: if prices[i+1] > prices[i], add the difference.

followUps: Can you prove this greedy approach is optimal?
parts.expectedApproach: Greedy: sum all consecutive positive differences.
